content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 22, 1990

Alan E. Ramos, Treasurer
California Business Credit PAC
973 Market Street, Ste. 600
San Francisco, CA 94103

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-280

Dear Mr. Ramos:

You have requested advice on behalf of the California Business Credit PAC concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your letter of April 10, 1990, your prior telephone call to this agency, and my call to you on May 5, 1990.

FACTS

California Business Credit PAC (CBC-PAC) registered as a general purpose recipient committee with the Secretary of State in 1986. The CBC-PAC has never contributed funds to any candidate or committees, nor does it contemplate doing so. The CBC-PAC uses its funds to monitor bills and does not participate in election campaigns.

QUESTION

Is California Business Credit PAC required to file campaign disclosure statements?

_______________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Alan E. Ramos
Page 2


CONCLUSION

The California Business Credit PAC is not required to file
campaign disclosure statements.

ANALYSIS

Section 82013(a) states that "committee" means any person or
combination of persons who directly or indirectly receives
contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year. Section
82015 and Regulation 18215 provide that a contribution is any
monetary or nonmonetary payment made for political purposes for
which full and adequate consideration is not made to the donor. A
payment is made for political purposes if it is:

...For the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence
the action of the voters for or against the nomination or
election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification
or passage of any measure.

Regulation 18215(a)(1).

According to the facts presented in your letter and in our
telephone conversations, it appears that the activities of CBC-PAC
have not resulted in it qualifying as a committee under the Act.
Therefore, it is not required to file campaign disclosure
statements (Form 420) and was not required to file a statement of
organization (Form 410). Enclosed is a statement of termination
(Form 415) which should be filed with the Secretary of State as
soon as possible.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Sandra L. Taylor
Political Reform Consultant

cc: Gail Jackson, Political Reform-SOS
Gloria Gilmore, Political Reform-SOS
